pattern: A palindrome is fully determined by its first half, so generating every half and mirroring it enumerates every k-palindromic candidate, and grouping candidates by their digit multiset avoids counting the same rearrangement family twice.
hints: Every palindrome of a fixed length is completely determined by choosing its first half (plus a middle digit when the length is odd), so you can enumerate all palindromes of that length by looping over every possible half.
For each half, mirror it into a full palindrome and check whether that number is divisible by k; if it is, the multiset of its digits describes an entire family of 'good' integers.

Different halves can mirror into palindromes that share the exact same digit multiset, so dedupe by the sorted digits before counting; then the number of good integers for a multiset is its total digit permutations minus the ones that start with zero.

edge_inputs.description: odd length with a mid-sized divisor
single-digit length, k picks out specific digits
odd length with a larger divisor
even length at a small size
k=1 makes every rearrangeable palindrome divisible, testing the leading-zero exclusion at scale
a divisor with no single-digit multiples forces multi-digit palindromes only

stress.description: random length and divisor pairs across the full constraint space
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 10)
    k = rng.randint(1, 9)
    return [n, k]
stress.note: single random (n, k) pair per generated case
